neighborhoods.name: Centro Historico
neighborhoods.description: Revitalized 19th-century port center with cathedral, Angela Peralta theater, and a vibrant arts scene
neighborhoods.character: Revitalized historic, arts scene, culinary culture
neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Mazatlán. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Zona Dorada (Golden Zone)
neighborhoods.description: Tourist beach strip with hotels, restaurants, and beachfront condominiums
neighborhoods.character: Beach tourist zone, condo lifestyle, resort amenities

housing.predominantType: Beachfront condos, apartments, and resort properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Mazatlán's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Playas del Sur
neighborhoods.description: Southern beach neighborhoods with newer development, lower costs, and local beach culture
neighborhoods.character: Emerging beach area, affordable, local character

housing.predominantType: New construction condos and modest houses

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Marina Mazatlan / El Cid
neighborhoods.description: Marina and golf resort area with established expat community, sports facilities, and ocean access
neighborhoods.character: Marina resort, golf and sports, expat social

housing.predominantType: Marina condos, golf community houses, and resort apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
